_comment: T190/T197/T198: non-regression coverage ratchet baseline, enforced by scripts/check-coverage-ratchet.sh in CI's coverage-report job. Numbers are LINE coverage percentages from reportgenerator's merged TextSummary. Backend: all four coverlet Cobertura reports merged, collected with `--settings backend/coverlet.runsettings` (T197) so EF migrations/Designer/ModelSnapshot files, obj/ generated build artifacts, and the Seeder tool assembly are excluded from the denominator. Frontend: flutter test --coverage's lcov.info, still unfiltered (see frontend.note). Raise these values only via a deliberate, standalone edit once coverage genuinely improves; never lower them.
_baselineSourceRule: T198 (2026-07-19): these numbers MUST be sourced from CI's OWN coverage artifact — download the coverage-report run's coverage-backend-*/coverage-frontend artifacts, merge with reportgenerator exactly as scripts/check-coverage-ratchet.sh does, and commit THOSE numbers — never from a local dotnet test / flutter test --coverage computation. The environment that ENFORCES the baseline (CI) must be the environment that PRODUCES it: local and CI measurements differ. See check-coverage-ratchet.sh's header for the full rule and the incident that established it.
_t197History: T197 (2026-07-19): re-measured backend UNFILTERED as of this commit at 20.2% (up from the stored 19.2% 2026-07-18 baseline, purely from this session's new test code — not a re-baselining decision on its own). Then measured FILTERED (backend/coverlet.runsettings applied) at 74.4% on the exact same LOCAL test run. The backend.lineCoveragePercent below is the FILTERED figure, now that CI collects with the settings file — this is a genuine methodology change, not a coverage improvement, and is not comparable to prior unfiltered numbers. ExcludeByAttribute (GeneratedCodeAttribute/CompilerGeneratedAttribute) was tested and rejected — see backend/coverlet.runsettings' own comment for why (it silently drops whole files of genuine record/primary-constructor-based production code in this codebase, confirmed by isolating each filter and comparing assembly/class/file counts). Fail-closed zero-coverable-lines behavior was re-verified (not assumed unchanged): forcibly excluded everything and confirmed scripts/check-coverage-ratchet.sh still hard-fails (exit 1, 'treating as inconclusive, not a genuine 0%').
_t198History: T198 (2026-07-19): T197's 74.4% backend baseline was computed LOCALLY (3229/4339 covered) and immediately tripped the ratchet on CI, which measured 3228/4339 (74.3%) on functionally-identical code — an IDENTICAL denominator differing by exactly ONE covered line (a background hosted-service timer path — outbox/search-refresh — that ran in the local measurement but not that CI run). Diagnosed as measurement drift, not an uncovered defensive line (the CI run that first PASSED, f6b3c2d, already contained both the T196 ConceptEndpoints visibility filter and the 74.4% baseline). Fix: re-baselined BOTH backend and frontend from CI's own coverage artifact (run 29671744916), and codified the CI-sourcing rule (see _baselineSourceRule). No epsilon/tolerance was added — rejected deliberately: a genuine drop, however small, gets a reviewed baseline update in its own commit; a standing tolerance would let small regressions accumulate silently.

backend.note: T198 CI-sourced baseline (from CI run 29671744916's coverage-backend-* artifacts, merged via reportgenerator exactly as the ratchet does): 3228/4339 coverable lines covered, 37 assemblies, backend/coverlet.runsettings filtering applied. Supersedes T197's LOCAL 74.4% (3229/4339) — same denominator, one fewer covered line, the drift that established the CI-sourcing rule.

frontend.note: T198 CI-sourced baseline (from CI run 29671744916's coverage-frontend/lcov.info, merged via reportgenerator): 2355/2941 coverable lines covered. Supersedes the prior LOCAL 78.9% (1895/2399, 2026-07-18, 87-case suite) — re-baselined from CI per the T198 sourcing rule; the rise reflects this session's new library-feature widget tests plus the larger current suite, a genuine improvement now locked in. Frontend remains unfiltered (no generated-client dilution — the generated Dart API client is a separate pub package flutter test --coverage never touches; frontend's own generated l10n/drift files stay included, as before).
